title: 3/3/20 Turn comments back on Polybius would say FOR KIDS

transcript.text: Hi, living in Boulder, V-Log, March 3rd. Right now it's March 4th, 2020 at 2.41 a.m.
I didn't do the video because I got way too hooked on my new project here.
Because yesterday I was going to look at my bike computer.
And I think I could have even ridden outside today, or tomorrow.
It's getting warm, so yeah, I'm super excited.
I have the Garmin 1030, and it has good features.
And I was looking at it today, and I went figuring it out.
And I'm looking at these Garmin Segment Maker, which is really easy.
For some reason, I never looked at it before.
And it just, yeah, it just takes you on the roads, and you can build them really fast.
And I think you can put, there's no limit to how many you can put on the phone.
So I'm going to make like 100 of them, just for the road riding.
I have an old mountain bike, so I'm not going to bother a mountain bike until I get a new mountain bike, I think.
But, yeah, it has, you know, and then I can then transfer it to my cell phone, which then I can transfer it over to my Garmin computer.
And on the Garmin computer, it says everything.
Now, if you want, if you go, hey, I'm going to go do the route, it tells you where to turn and everything.
And it tells you how far until a climb, how long the climb is, what the percentage of the climb is.
And then it, and then I guess you can put up to like 100 Strava segments onto it.
I kept downloading it, but then having to download again.
So I don't know, maybe if you star them, maybe whatever you star just pops up on your phone, on your computer.
So, yeah, I just want to see how that works, go on an actual ride.
But I've been hooked on building these segments.
I've made like 50 of them so far.
And it's just, I made a whole bunch of them with my, with Google Earth before.
But Google Earth making them is really slow.
And it took me forever to build them on that.
And, yeah, I like to make the ones that I have on Google Earth because I spent so much time to make good ones to transfer them over to this.
But I'm just making them off of this here.
And, um, and, uh, it's, uh, I mean, I'm like getting super, super excited about doing the ride because, um, then you can turn it into a Strava segment.
Um, I have it, my segment, it comes from my house, but you can make it a Strava segment from leading like city limits.
And, um, it's, uh, um, yeah, I mean, I've made like 50.
I just want to, I probably stay up all night long.
Um, I was drinking, got an apple cider vinegar.
My roommate gave me weed last night.
I've been smoking and eating junk food.
I had like a, went in for an omelet this morning, a Dodd's diner, because I dropped my bike off to get fixed.
The shifters weren't working to get the handlebars put flat.
And, um, then I got five guys burgers and fries when I took my computer in.
They had to send that back in to get, to get the new battery put in.
Um, and then I got, yeah, the, the, the drink.
And then I, and then I got an energy drink, a $3 bag of candy and, um, Doritos.
So, cause I was just like, I'm just, this is freaking me out.
Um, it's crazy.
Um, yeah.
And so I guess I'll make more when I get a mountain bike, I'll make more, but yeah, I want to make a hundred and just road ones, just all kinds.
And I am including some like dirt sections into it, so I think my road bike can work okay as a dirt bike.
I'll see how big of tires I can put on it.
But yeah, I'm just put doing everything on here, the short ones, long ones.
So I could probably do a whole hundred on here.
And, uh, and then, yeah, just see how it goes.
Just go and ride around and explore as soon as I can.
But I think tomorrow I'll probably be working on this.
So I'll take two more days off just cause I'm trying to get my bike computer going.
But, um, yeah, I want to do it full, full on.
So, um, yeah, I still haven't even had time to figure out how to like, you know, move the screen around and stuff.
So, um, this is exciting.
Um, so, uh, yeah, but I mean, my legs were sore today.
Okay, so, um, cause yesterday I, yeah, I like, yeah, I swam 2K, 1.9K on the Erg machine, biked 56 miles inside, and then ran 3.1 miles and squatted all the sums.
So that was a lot of exercise for me.
Anyway, um, but if I don't do anything tomorrow, I'll definitely be ready to go to after tomorrow, like outside.
Okay, enough talking.
Um, yeah, maybe, um, oh, and then another thing I want to do is put it on my website.
I want to put it on, like, organized, all the ones that I made, that I have here, that I made on this.
Um, just copy and paste it.
I could probably do it really quickly, but I want to organize it, cause this thing organizes it for you, in terms of how, the longest to shortest.
But I also want to organize it from, um, highest to lowest.
And I think you can do that also, with the help of the filter, to do it quickly.
And then I also want to organize it, um, highest to lowest, in terms of, um, climbing per mile.
I don't, I don't know, I don't know if I've ever seen that in a course before, but I think that's a really awesome metric to use.
It's how much climbing it actually has, and, and for how, in ratio for how far of a ride it is, you know.
So I'm super psyched to look at that.
So I, I think I'll do a hundred, just a cool hundred.
Somebody, I, I googled how many could fit on, and yeah, they said you could fit a hundred Strava segments.
But some guys were like, yeah, I don't think they have an official limit for this, the, uh, roots.
And some guy said he had 70, and that was, and it was still working.
So yeah, I'll just do a hundred, because that's a good even number.
And then put that on my, uh, my website and make that, because I don't think it'll take that much longer.
So that's the, yeah, side of the psyched about doing that.
That'll take all day tomorrow.
So yeah, I think I'll be back on the horse after tomorrow.
All right, talk to you till later.
